title: Katie Boulter Loses Tennis Match in Austria
summary: Katie Boulter, a tennis player from Britain, lost her first match at a tournament in Austria. She played against Elena-Gabriela Ruse from Romania. The match was very close, with both sets decided by tie-breaks. Boulter tried hard, breaking Ruse's serve a few times, but Ruse won 7-6, 7-6. Boulter is ranked 62nd in the world and has won a title earlier this year. This loss means she is out of the tournament early. The article talks about her recent performances in other tournaments too.

background_read: Tennis is a popular sport played worldwide. Players compete in tournaments to win titles and improve their rankings. A match involves sets and games, and sometimes tie-breaks are used to decide close sets. Katie Boulter is a professional tennis player from Britain who has had some successes in her career.
Article_Structure: WHO: Katie Boulter, a British tennis player, and Elena-Gabriela Ruse, a Romanian tennis player.
WHAT: Boulter lost her first-round match to Ruse in a tournament in Austria.
WHERE: The match happened at the Upper Austria Ladies Linz Open in Austria.
WHY: The article reports on Boulter's early exit from the tournament and her recent performances.
